feedback.text: The organisation advertisers in the Trade Mark Association (OWM) considers that consumer protection on the internet is sufficiently addressed by the existing legislation in the form of General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR), Digital Services Act (DSA), Digital Market Act (DMA), Unfair Commercial Practices Directive (UCPD), Consumers Right Directive (CRD) and Audiovisual Media Services Directive (AVMSD). The OWM believes that the European Commission should focus on the uniform enforcement, harmonisation and application of these existing rules.
